# Figure description: 4_Карта_прогноов_M-42-VIII.jpg

**Source**: `36572_Smolianova_1984/raw_graphics/M-42-VIII/4_Карта_прогноов_M-42-VIII.jpg`  
**Generated by**: claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## Figure Type

This is a **geological forecast/prognosis map** ("Карта прогнозов") with elements showing the spatial distribution patterns of mineral deposits. It is a thematic geological map at 1:200,000 scale, combining lithological-structural mapping with mineral occurrence predictions. The map uses a combination of color fills, hatching patterns, contour lines, and point symbols to convey multiple layers of geological and metallogenic information.

---

## Coverage and Subject

- **Map sheet:** М-42-VIII (printed in the upper right of the map frame)
- **Scale:** 1:200,000
- **Year:** 1984 г. (1984)
- **Organization:** Министерство геологии Казахской ССР, Центрально-Казахстанское производственное геологическое объединение
- **Compiler:** Нагменко В.В. (Nagmenko V.V.)
- **Compiled at:** Центральная геолого-поисковая экспедиция (Central Geological Prospecting Expedition)
- **Archive stamp:** Казахская ССР, Республиканский геологический фонд, Инв. № 36572

The map covers a portion of northern Kazakhstan corresponding to the М-42-VIII sheet. The area contains a complex of Precambrian (Archean and Proterozoic) crystalline basement rocks exposed in inliers, overlain by Cenozoic (KZ) cover sequences.

---

## Key Content

### Geological Units and Mapping

**Stratigraphic/lithological units visible** (based on map labels):
- **KZ** — Cenozoic cover (widespread, shown in unornamented white/blank areas across much of the map, especially in the central and peripheral zones)
- **P₁Am** / **P₂Am** — Proterozoic (likely Lower and Upper Proterozoic) metamorphic/crystalline formations; these labels recur throughout the map in numerous small inliers and larger exposures
- **P₁Ar** / **P₂Ar** — Archean formations, appearing in clusters particularly in the southern and eastern portions of the map
- **P₁βb** / **P₂βb** — likely Proterozoic basic intrusive or metavolcanic units
- **C₁βn** — possibly Lower Carboniferous basic rocks
- **P₁δb** — Proterozoic dioritic or basic intrusive rocks

### Structural Features
- **Contour lines** (isohypses or depth contours to basement) run across the map, with values labeled at intervals: 600, 800, 1000, 1200, 1400, 1600, 1800, 2000, 2200, 2400 metres. These likely represent the depth to crystalline basement beneath Cenozoic cover, with the deepest values (~2400 m) in the southwestern corner and shallower values (<600 m) in the eastern and central-eastern parts. This reveals a **basement depression** deepening to the southwest and a **basement high** in the northeast/east.
- **Dashed contour lines** indicate inferred/interpolated structure beneath cover.
- **Solid curvilinear lines** likely represent geological boundaries or fault traces.

### Mineral Occurrences and Forecast Zones

**Point symbols** (mineral occurrences):
- **Solid black dots** with element labels: Cu (copper), Au (gold), Ag (silver), Mo (molybdenum), Ta (tantalum?), and others — these mark known mineral occurrences or deposits
- **Open circle dots** similarly labeled with Cu, Au, etc. — likely prospects or geochemical anomalies of lesser certainty
- Notable clusters of **Cu** occurrences appear in the south-central and southeastern portions of the map
- **Au/Ag** occurrences appear in the northern portion

**Forecast/prognosis zones** (colored and hatched areas):
- **Upper right (northeast):** A large zone shown with **grey fill and diagonal black hatching** (cross-hatching in two directions), overlaid with **red diagonal hatching**. This is clearly a high-priority forecast zone or a known ore district. The label nearby reads **"В Со 432·10⁶"** — likely indicating a **prognostic resource estimate of 432 × 10⁶ units** (possibly tonnes of ore or metal content) for cobalt (Co) or copper-cobalt mineralization in a Category B or inferred resource classification.
- **Left/west side:** A zone with **red horizontal hatching** (parallel horizontal red lines) extending along the western margin, particularly prominent in the southwestern quadrant. This likely represents a separate forecast zone for a different commodity or a different confidence category.
- **Left-center:** Label **"IБ 76·10⁶"** — another prognostic resource estimate of **76 × 10⁶ units**, Category IБ (a Soviet resource classification category).
- **Label "ЗГ"** in large text in the west-central area — likely a named geological zone or structural block designation.
- **Green diagonal line symbols** scattered across the map — these likely represent **fault zones, shear zones, or ore-controlling structures** favorable for mineralization (a standard Soviet metallogenic map symbol for predicted ore-controlling lineaments or prospective zones).

### Contour Pattern Interpretation
The basement depth contours define:
- A **NW-SE trending depression axis** in the western half of the map (depths 1400–2400 m)
- A **basement high** in the east-northeast (depths <800 m), coinciding with the large hatched forecast zone
- Intermediate depths (800–1200 m) in the central area
